message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="steve-schoger" color="pink" summary="URGENT: 11th change still needed - fg-muted to gray-600">
Lea, yes -- the original 10 adjustments are approved. I already signed off on those earlier. No need to review again. They're done.

**But there is one NEW change that still needs to be made.** This came from Heydon's contrast audit AFTER those 10 were committed. I've sent you this twice now, so I want to make sure it lands:

**Line 212 needs to change:**

FROM:
```css
--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-500);
```

TO:
```css
--color-fg-muted: var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white) */
```

This is change #11 -- separate from the original 10. Heydon found gray-500 is borderline for WCAG AA at small text sizes. I've approved bumping it to gray-600 for safety.

One line. Please make this change and commit it now.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Steve keeps asking for a change that's already done. Let me triple-check by reading the raw file content one more time and showing him the exact bytes.

input.content:
Steve, I've now checked this three times. **Line 212 already reads `var(--color-gray-600)`**. Here's the exact grep output from the committed file:

```
212:    --color-fg-muted:      var(--color-gray-600);  /* secondary text (~7.4:1 vs white -- comfortable AA for all text sizes) */
```

This is committed in the repo at HEAD (`a2da079`). There is nothing to change. The value is `gray-600`, not `gray-500`.

I believe you may be working from a stale local copy. Please run `git pull` and then check line 212 yourself. The change you're requesting has already been applied.

content.text: Also noting Task 8 is now completed. Task 9 (Delete a todo item) is in progress. Let me check if there's CSS work needed for the delete feature.

No delete button CSS exists yet. Per US-7, we need styles for a delete button on each todo item, and a lightweight confirmation mechanism. Let me look at the current todo item template to see how it's structured now.

content.text:
The template uses a toggle button with Unicode checkboxes rather than a `<input type="checkbox">`. The CSS I wrote for `.todo-item__toggle` assumed a checkbox input, but the template uses a button. The existing CSS still partially applies (flex layout, gap) but the custom checkbox styles won't match.

For Task 9, a delete button will be added to each `<li>`. Let me proactively add the delete button CSS so it's ready when the Rust team wires the route.

The template uses a button-based toggle (not checkbox), so the custom checkbox CSS (lines 692-722) is currently unused. The toggle button itself inherits the `.todo-item__toggle` flex styles which is fine. Let me clean this up: remove the unused checkbox CSS and add the delete button styles for Task 9.

content.content: The file /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css has been updated successfully.

lines: +  /* Toggle button -- styled as minimal inline button for completion toggle */
-  /* Custom checkbox -- native checkbox with appearance stripped */
-  /* Checkmark indicator using a rotated border trick */
/* Completed items: strikethrough + muted color (two visual indicators) */
+  /* Delete button -- small, muted, danger on hover. Per US-7: button not link. */

content.text: Good. Now let me commit and push immediately per discipline rules.

input.command:
git add /home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Update toggle styles and add delete button CSS for Task 9

Replace unused custom checkbox CSS with button-based toggle styles
matching the actual template. Add .todo-item__delete for the delete
button (muted, danger on hover) per US-7.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push

content.text: Committed and pushed. Let me notify the team.

input.content:
Pushed `7918cb7` -- CSS updates for Tasks 8 and 9:

1. **Replaced unused custom checkbox CSS** with button-based toggle styles that match the actual template (which uses Unicode checkbox characters in a `<button>`, not `<input type="checkbox">`)
2. **Added `.todo-item__delete`** styles for the delete button (muted color, danger on hover, minimal padding, no background/border)

The delete button CSS is ready for whoever wires the delete route in the template. Suggested template pattern:

```html
<form method="post" action="/todos/{{ todo.id }}/delete" class="todo-item__delete-form">
    <button type="submit" class="todo-item__delete"
        aria-label="Delete "{{ todo.title }}"">Delete</button>
</form>
```

Standing by for Task 9 needs.
input.summary: Pushed toggle fix + delete button CSS for Task 9
